revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年}}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名}} in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano Ward|ja|中野区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa City|ja|桶川市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|東区 (福岡市)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島}} - An island in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line|ja|阪神高速3号神戸線}} in {{ill|Suma Ward, Kobe City|ja|須磨区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路}} in {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
== Railway == * {{ill|Wakamiya Station|ja|若宮駅}} - A station on the {{ill|JR Tadami Line|ja|只見線}} in {{ill|Aizubange Town|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Kawanuma District|ja|河沼郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍}} warships or {{ill|Kaibokan|ja|海防艦}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|ja|若宮 (水上機母艦)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class Kaibokan|ja|択捉型海防艦}}.</ref>
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{Prefix}} * {{intitle}} * {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hall|ja|若宮館}} * {{ill|Shingū (disambiguation)|ja|新宮 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja|今宮 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮}} * {{ill|Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja|元宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名}} in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano Ward|ja|中野区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa City|ja|桶川市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|東区 (福岡市)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島}} - An island in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line|ja|阪神高速3号神戸線}} in {{ill|Suma Ward, Kobe City|ja|須磨区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路}} in {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
== Railway == * {{ill|Wakamiya Station|ja|若宮駅}} - A station on the {{ill|JR Tadami Line|ja|只見線}} in {{ill|Aizubange Town|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Kawanuma District|ja|河沼郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍}} warships or {{ill|Kaibokan|ja|海防艦}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|ja|若宮 (水上機母艦)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class Kaibokan|ja|択捉型海防艦}}.</ref>

'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名}} in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano Ward|ja|中野区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa City|ja|桶川市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|東区 (福岡市)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島}} - An island in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line|ja|阪神高速3号神戸線}} in {{ill|Suma Ward, Kobe City|ja|須磨区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路}} in {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.

== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍}} warships or {{ill|Kaibokan|ja|海防艦}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|ja|若宮 (水上機母艦)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class Kaibokan|ja|択捉型海防艦}}.</ref>

A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名}} in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano Ward|ja|中野区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa City|ja|桶川市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|東区 (福岡市)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島}} - An island in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line|ja|阪神高速3号神戸線}} in {{ill|Suma Ward, Kobe City|ja|須磨区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路}} in {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
== Railway == * {{ill|Wakamiya Station|ja|若宮駅}} - A station on the {{ill|JR Tadami Line|ja|只見線}} in {{ill|Aizubange Town|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Kawanuma District|ja|河沼郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍}} warships or {{ill|Kaibokan|ja|海防艦}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|ja|若宮 (水上機母艦)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class Kaibokan|ja|択捉型海防艦}}.</ref>
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{Prefix}} * {{intitle}} * {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hall|ja|若宮館}} * {{ill|Shingū (disambiguation)|ja|新宮 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja|今宮 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮}} * {{ill|Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja|元宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名}} in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano Ward|ja|中野区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa City|ja|桶川市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|東区 (福岡市)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島}} - An island in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line|ja|阪神高速3号神戸線}} in {{ill|Suma Ward, Kobe City|ja|須磨区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路}} in {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
== Railway == * {{ill|Wakamiya Station|ja|若宮駅}} - A station on the {{ill|JR Tadami Line|ja|只見線}} in {{ill|Aizubange Town|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Kawanuma District|ja|河沼郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍}} warships or {{ill|Kaibokan|ja|海防艦}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|ja|若宮 (水上機母艦)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class Kaibokan|ja|択捉型海防艦}}.</ref>
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{Prefix}} * {{intitle}} * {{ill|Miyawaka City|ja|宮若市}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hall|ja|若宮館}} * {{ill|Shingū (disambiguation)|ja|新宮 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja|今宮 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮}} * {{ill|Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja|元宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名}} in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano Ward|ja|中野区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa City|ja|桶川市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara City|ja|市原市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)}} - '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|ja|1947th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22nd year }}), when the {{ill|Eleven Miyake|ja|旧皇族}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Hiroyasu Fushimi|ja|伏見宮博恭王}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|ja|伏見宮}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Tomohito|ja|寬仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Takahito of Mikasa|ja|三笠宮崇仁親王}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Hisahito|ja|悠仁親王}}, the first son of {{ill|Prince Fumihito|ja|秋篠宮文仁親王}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial Household|ja|皇室}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|ja|若宮大路}} - The {{ill|approach|ja|参道}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|qq=|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|qq=|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|qq=|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|qq=|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|qq=|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|qq=|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|qq=|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|qq=|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * [[:en:Wakamiya Ōji|Wakamiya Ōji]] - The [[:en:Sandō|approach]] to [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/locality|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|qq=|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13481699}} - A locality in [[:en:Nakano, Tokyo|Nakano Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|qq=|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616242}} - A locality in [[:en:Okegawa, Saitama|Okegawa City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|qq=|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616240}} - A locality in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara City]], Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|qq=|1=Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|qq=|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in [[:en:Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]] - A former municipality in [[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]], Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of [[:en:Miyawaka|Miyawaka City]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|qq=|1=Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616276}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]]. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮村|1=Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616272}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Island|qq=|1=Wakamiya Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616271}} - An island in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya River|qq=|1=Wakamiya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408479}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/river|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川|1=river|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4022}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=xing|WD=Q61942409}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirofumi Wakamiya|qq=|1=Yoshibumi Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616265}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiko Wakamiya|qq=|1=Teiko Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616244}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Interchange|qq=|1=Wakamiya Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616262}} - An interchange on the [[:en:Kobe Route|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line]] in [[:en:Suma-ku, Kobe|Suma Ward, Kobe City]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Boulevard|qq=|1=Wakamiya-odori Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616268}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-meter road|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Street|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釜座通|1=Kamanza Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648096}} - A north-south road in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]].
== Railway == * [[:en:Wakamiya Station|Wakamiya Station]] - A station on the [[:en:Tadami Line|JR Tadami Line]] in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange Town]], [[:en:Kawanuma District, Fukushima|Kawanuma District]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q219712}} warships or [[:en:Kaibōkan|Kaibokan]]. * [[:en:Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)]] * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|qq=|1=Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753012}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the [[:en:Etorofu-class escort ship|Etorofu-class Kaibokan]].</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|qq=|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|qq=|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|qq=|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|qq=|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|qq=|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}

Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * [[:en:Wakamiya Ōji|Wakamiya Ōji]] - The [[:en:Sandō|approach]] to [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}

Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * [[:en:Wakamiya Ōji|Wakamiya Ōji]] - The [[:en:Sandō|approach]] to [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * [[:en:Wakamiya Ōji|Wakamiya Ōji]] - The [[:en:Sandō|approach]] to [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

A locality in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * [[:en:Wakamiya Ōji|Wakamiya Ōji]] - The [[:en:Sandō|approach]] to [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/locality|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13481699}} - A locality in [[:en:Nakano, Tokyo|Nakano Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616242}} - A locality in [[:en:Okegawa, Saitama|Okegawa City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616240}} - A locality in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara City]], Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|1=Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in [[:en:Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]] - A former municipality in [[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]], Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of [[:en:Miyawaka|Miyawaka City]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|1=Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616276}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]]. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮村|1=Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616272}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Island|1=Wakamiya Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616271}} - An island in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya River|1=Wakamiya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408479}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川|1=river|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4022}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=xing|WD=Q61942409}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirofumi Wakamiya|1=Yoshibumi Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616265}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiko Wakamiya|1=Teiko Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616244}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Interchange|1=Wakamiya Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616262}} - An interchange on the [[:en:Kobe Route|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line]] in [[:en:Suma-ku, Kobe|Suma Ward, Kobe City]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Boulevard|1=Wakamiya-odori Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616268}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-meter road|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釜座通|1=Kamanza Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648096}} - A north-south road in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]].
== Railway == * [[:en:Wakamiya Station|Wakamiya Station]] - A station on the [[:en:Tadami Line|JR Tadami Line]] in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange Town]], [[:en:Kawanuma District, Fukushima|Kawanuma District]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q219712}} warships or [[:en:Kaibōkan|Kaibokan]]. * [[:en:Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)]] * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|1=Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753012}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the [[:en:Etorofu-class escort ship|Etorofu-class Kaibokan]].</ref>

== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}.
