<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before [[:en:1947|1947]] ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the [[:en:Ōke|Eleven Miyake]] renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince [[:en:Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|Hiroyasu Fushimi]] before inheriting the [[:en:Fushimi-no-miya|Fushimi-no-miya]] title. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * [[:en:Wakamiya Ōji|Wakamiya Ōji]] - The [[:en:Sandō|approach]] to [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/locality|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13481699}} - A locality in [[:en:Nakano, Tokyo|Nakano Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616242}} - A locality in [[:en:Okegawa, Saitama|Okegawa City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616240}} - A locality in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara City]], Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|1=Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in [[:en:Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. * [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]] - A former municipality in [[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]], Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of [[:en:Miyawaka|Miyawaka City]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|1=Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616276}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]]. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮村|1=Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616272}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Island|1=Wakamiya Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616271}} - An island in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya River|1=Wakamiya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408479}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川|1=river|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4022}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=xing|WD=Q61942409}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirofumi Wakamiya|1=Yoshibumi Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616265}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiko Wakamiya|1=Teiko Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616244}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Interchange|1=Wakamiya Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616262}} - An interchange on the [[:en:Kobe Route|Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line]] in [[:en:Suma-ku, Kobe|Suma Ward, Kobe City]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Boulevard|1=Wakamiya-odori Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616268}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-meter road|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釜座通|1=Kamanza Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648096}} - A north-south road in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]].
== Railway == * [[:en:Wakamiya Station|Wakamiya Station]] - A station on the [[:en:Tadami Line|JR Tadami Line]] in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange Town]], [[:en:Kawanuma District, Fukushima|Kawanuma District]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q219712}} warships or [[:en:Kaibōkan|Kaibokan]]. * [[:en:Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)]] * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|1=Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753012}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the [[:en:Etorofu-class escort ship|Etorofu-class Kaibokan]].</ref>

== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. He succeeded the title in [[:en:1923|1923]].</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince [[:en:Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|Tomohito]], the first son of [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Prince Takahito of Mikasa]], would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Hisahito]], the first son of [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]], would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/locality|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} in {{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|WD=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13481699}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano, Tokyo|en|Nakano, Tokyo|WD=Q234087|lt=Nakano Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616242}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa, Saitama|en|Okegawa, Saitama|WD=Q570333|lt=Okegawa City}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616240}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|WD=Q207881|lt=Ichihara City}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|1=Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|en|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206659|lt=Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|en|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|WD=Q1639231|lt=Wakamiya Town}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka City}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|1=Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616276}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮村|1=Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616272}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Island|1=Wakamiya Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616271}} - An island in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya River|1=Wakamiya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408479}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川|1=river|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4022}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100249640]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/locality|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} in {{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|WD=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13481699}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano, Tokyo|en|Nakano, Tokyo|WD=Q234087|lt=Nakano Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616242}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa, Saitama|en|Okegawa, Saitama|WD=Q570333|lt=Okegawa City}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616240}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|WD=Q207881|lt=Ichihara City}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|1=Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|en|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206659|lt=Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|en|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|WD=Q1639231|lt=Wakamiya Town}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka City}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|1=Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616276}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮村|1=Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616272}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Island|1=Wakamiya Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616271}} - An island in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya River|1=Wakamiya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408479}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川|1=river|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4022}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=xing|WD=Q61942409}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirofumi Wakamiya|1=Yoshibumi Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616265}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiko Wakamiya|1=Teiko Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616244}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Interchange|1=Wakamiya Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616262}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Kobe Route|en|Kobe Route|WD=Q11656969|lt=Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line}} in {{ill|Suma-ku, Kobe|en|Suma-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1155010|lt=Suma Ward, Kobe City}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Boulevard|1=Wakamiya-odori Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616268}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-meter road|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釜座通|1=Kamanza Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648096}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}.
== Railway == * {{ill|Wakamiya Station|en|Wakamiya Station|WD=Q7960796|lt=Wakamiya Station}} - A station on the {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|WD=Q1054717|lt=JR Tadami Line}} in {{ill|Aizubange|en|Aizubange|WD=Q1204988|lt=Aizubange Town}}, {{ill|Kawanuma District, Fukushima|en|Kawanuma District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155856|lt=Kawanuma District}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q219712}} warships or {{ill|Kaibōkan|en|Kaibōkan|WD=Q2599160|lt=Kaibokan}}. * {{ill|Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|en|Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|WD=Q3491540|lt=Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|1=Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753012}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class escort ship|en|Etorofu-class escort ship|WD=Q3791913|lt=Etorofu-class Kaibokan}}.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{Prefix}} * {{intitle}} * {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka City}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hall|ja|若宮館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingū (disambiguation)|ja|新宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新宮|1=Shingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475200}} * {{ill|Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja|今宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 今宮|1=Imamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376856}} * {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja|元宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元宮|1=Motomiya (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97181444}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]]
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Hongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]]
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake|1=Ōke}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi|1=Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya|1=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa|1=Takahito, Prince Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito|1=Prince Hisahito of Akishino}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito|1=Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Q11520115|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji|1=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach|1=Sandō}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

A locality in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|WD=Q207881|lt=Ichihara City|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|1=Q11465321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]]
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake|1=Ōke}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi|1=Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya|1=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa|1=Takahito, Prince Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito|1=Prince Hisahito of Akishino}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito|1=Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Q11520115|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji|1=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach|1=Sandō}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q219712}} warships or {{ill|Kaibōkan|en|Kaibōkan|WD=Q2599160|lt=Kaibokan|1=Kaibōkan}}. * {{ill|Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|en|Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|WD=Q3491540|lt=Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|1=Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|1=Q48753012|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753012}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class escort ship|en|Etorofu-class escort ship|WD=Q3791913|lt=Etorofu-class Kaibokan|1=Etorofu-class escort ship}}.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{Prefix}} * {{intitle}} * {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka City|1=Miyawaka}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hall|ja|若宮館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingū (disambiguation)|ja|新宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新宮|1=Shingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475200}} * {{ill|Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja|今宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 今宮|1=Imamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376856}} * {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja|元宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元宮|1=Q97181444|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97181444}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Wakamiya]][[fr:Wakamiya]][[ja:若宮]][[ko:와카미야]]
'''Wakamiya''' (若宮) {{TOCright}}
== Imperial Household == * A child of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Refers to a {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} during their childhood. * Before {{ill|1947|en|1947|WD=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} ({{ill|Shōwa 22|ja|昭和22年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 22|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1947年|1=1947|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5263}}), when the {{ill|Ōke|en|Ōke|WD=Q864190|lt=Eleven Miyake|1=Ōke}} renounced their {{ill|Imperial status|ja|皇籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial status|1=imperial status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581092}}, it was customary to refer to the heir of a {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844727}} head (usually the eldest son) as "'''○○ Wakamiya''' (○○のわかみや)." <ref>May 4, 1895, Yomiuri Shimbun: "The 5th Selected Exhibition Awards Ceremony - Prizes Presented by Kitashirakawa Wakamiya Prince"</ref><ref>May 25, 1907, Asahi Shimbun: "Naming of Fushimi Wakamiya Royal Daughter" Here, "Fushimi Wakamiya" refers to Prince {{ill|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|en|Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu|WD=Q285846|lt=Hiroyasu Fushimi|1=Prince Fushimi Hiroyasu}} before inheriting the {{ill|Fushimi-no-miya|en|Fushimi-no-miya|WD=Q1038337|lt=Fushimi-no-miya|1=Fushimi-no-miya}} title. He succeeded the title in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}}.</ref><ref>December 24, 1941, Yomiuri Shimbun: "Kitashirakawa Wakamiya attends the Children's Music Event"</ref>. According to this custom, Prince {{ill|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|en|Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|WD=Q486381|lt=Tomohito|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa}}, the first son of {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Prince Takahito of Mikasa|1=Takahito, Prince Mikasa}}, would be "'''Mikasa Wakamiya'''", and Prince {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Hisahito|1=Prince Hisahito of Akishino}}, the first son of {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito|1=Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan}}, would be "'''Akishino Wakamiya'''." However, this designation is rarely used even among those knowledgeable about the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} system today. Instead, it is common to use the {{ill|Miyake|ja|宮号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12250872}} as if it were a {{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}. Prince Tomohito expressed his criticism of this practice. {{main|{{ill|Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|ja|寬仁親王#宮号の有無と表記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tomohito#Miyake naming conventions|1=Prince Tomohito of Mikasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q486381}}}}
== Religion == * A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrining the child ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}}) of the main deity ({{ill|Hongen|ja|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongen|1=Q11520115|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520115}}). Examples include various {{ill|Wakamiya Shrines|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrines|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} and {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|ja|若宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrines|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|WD=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji|1=Wakamiya Ōji}} - The {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=approach|1=Sandō}} to {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮神社|1=Wakamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja|若宮社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Sha (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡宮|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}
== Place Names == Japanese place names. * Wakamiya (Yamagata City) - A {{ill|locality|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/locality|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Toponym|WD=Q7884789}} in {{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|WD=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata (city)}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|ja|若宮 (中野区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Nakano Ward)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13481699}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakano, Tokyo|en|Nakano, Tokyo|WD=Q234087|lt=Nakano Ward|1=Nakano, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|ja|若宮 (桶川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Okegawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616242}} - A locality in {{ill|Okegawa, Saitama|en|Okegawa, Saitama|WD=Q570333|lt=Okegawa City|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|ja|若宮 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Ichikawa City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616240}} - A locality in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City|1=Ichikawa, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. * Wakamiya (Ichihara City) - A locality in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|WD=Q207881|lt=Ichihara City|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Wakamiya (Nagano City) - A locality in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. {{main|{{ill|Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|ja|尾張部 (長野市)#若宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owabebe (Nagano City)#Wakamiya|1=Q11465321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465321}}}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja|若宮 (佐賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Saga City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City|1=Saga (city)}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|ja|若宮 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Fukuoka City)|1=Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111009717}} - A locality in {{ill|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|en|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206659|lt=Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City|1=Higashi-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|en|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|WD=Q1639231|lt=Wakamiya Town|1=Wakamiya, Fukuoka}} - A former municipality in {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, now part of {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka City|1=Miyawaka}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|ja|若宮町 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (disambiguation)|1=Q11616276|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616276}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja|若宮町 (金沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Kanazawa City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja|若宮町 (名古屋市中村区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Town (Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A locality in {{ill|Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市中村区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Ward, Nagoya City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City|1=Nagoya}}. * Wakamiya Town (Yamaguchi City) - A locality in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City|1=Yamaguchi (city)}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja|若宮村 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Village (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮村|1=Q11616272|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616272}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Island|ja|若宮島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Island|1=Q11616271|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616271}} - An island in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya River|ja|若宮川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya River|1=Wakamiya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408479}} - A {{ill|river|ja|河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川|1=River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4022}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City|1=Nihonmatsu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
== Personal Names == A Japanese {{ill|surname|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Chinese ancestral surname|WD=Q61942409}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Clan|ja|若宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hirofumi Wakamiya|ja|若宮啓文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirofumi Wakamiya|1=Yoshibumi Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616265}} - Journalist. * {{ill|Teiko Wakamiya|ja|若宮テイ子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiko Wakamiya|1=Teiko Wakamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616244}} - Disc jockey.
== Transportation == * {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Interchange|1=Q11616262|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616262}} - An interchange on the {{ill|Kobe Route|en|Kobe Route|WD=Q11656969|lt=Hanshin Expressway Route 3 Kobe Line|1=Kobe Route}} in {{ill|Suma-ku, Kobe|en|Suma-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1155010|lt=Suma Ward, Kobe City|1=Suma-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Boulevard|ja|若宮大通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Boulevard|1=Wakamiya-odori Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616268}} - A {{ill|100-meter road|ja|100m道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-meter road|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Street|ja|若宮通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釜座通|1=Kamanza Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648096}} - A north-south road in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}.
== Railway == * {{ill|Wakamiya Station|en|Wakamiya Station|WD=Q7960796|lt=Wakamiya Station|1=Wakamiya Station}} - A station on the {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|WD=Q1054717|lt=JR Tadami Line|1=Tadami Line}} in {{ill|Aizubange|en|Aizubange|WD=Q1204988|lt=Aizubange Town|1=Aizubange}}, {{ill|Kawanuma District, Fukushima|en|Kawanuma District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155856|lt=Kawanuma District|1=Kawanuma District, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
== Military == Japanese {{ill|Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q219712}} warships or {{ill|Kaibōkan|en|Kaibōkan|WD=Q2599160|lt=Kaibokan|1=Kaibōkan}}. * {{ill|Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|en|Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya|WD=Q3491540|lt=Wakamiya (Seaplane Tender)|1=Japanese seaplane carrier Wakamiya}} * {{ill|Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|ja|若宮 (海防艦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya (Kaibokan)|1=Q48753012|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753012}} <ref>The second Wakamiya, the 8th ship of the {{ill|Etorofu-class escort ship|en|Etorofu-class escort ship|WD=Q3791913|lt=Etorofu-class Kaibokan|1=Etorofu-class escort ship}}.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{Prefix}} * {{intitle}} * {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka City|1=Miyawaka}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hall|ja|若宮館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingū (disambiguation)|ja|新宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新宮|1=Shingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475200}} * {{ill|Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja|今宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imamiya (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 今宮|1=Imamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376856}} * {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja|元宮 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元宮|1=Q97181444|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97181444}}
